_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: FL locals batch — St. Petersburg D4 / D3 / D8 (Hanewicz, Harting, Floyd) + Tallahassee Seats 2 / 5 (Richardson, Williams-Cox). Rosters web-verified June 2026 against talgov.com + stpete.org + Ballotpedia + Florida Politics. ANCHOR votes used as source of truth across the batch: (1) Oct 9 2025 St Pete Harmony+History flag resolution — Harting was the LONE NO; resolution withdrawn before final roll call but the committee/procedural posture is recorded (biblical_marriage q4 TRUE for Harting; FALSE for Floyd who actively elevated it; Hanewicz FALSE as Council Chair who continued to deliver Pride flag-raising remarks at 2025+2026 ceremonies). (2) FY2025-26 St Pete budget adopted at 6.4525 millage, 3.93% above rollback — only Driscoll + Givens dissented (Givens for MORE spending); Hanewicz / Harting / Floyd all AYE without restraint advocacy = economic_stewardship q2 FALSE for all three. (3) Oct 22 2025 Tallahassee 287(g) ICE rescission motion FAILED 3-2 — Richardson + Williams-Cox + Mayor Dailey AYE to KEEP cooperation = border_immigration q2 TRUE for both (even where rhetoric framed it as reluctant state-law compliance). DATA-CLEANUP FLAG: scorecard listed Williams-Cox as 'City Commissioner, Seat 1' but the talgov.com bio + 2026 candidate filings + WCTV election coverage confirm she holds SEAT 5 (Matlow holds Seat 3, Porter holds Seat 5 in the scorecard is also likely mis-labeled — defer to a future batch when Matlow / Porter are processed). Office text updated to Seat 5 with the correction noted. HIGH-BARS HONORED: public_justice q0 (affirmative sustained pro-LE advocacy beyond a budget vote) — Richardson cleared via FY24 millage-hike specifically to fund 20 added TPD officers + Nov 2025 YES on $625K federal hire-grant; Williams-Cox cleared via FL PBA Big Bend Chapter Mar 2026 endorsement citing her sustained record of approving negotiated PBA contracts; Hanewicz did NOT clear despite 2021 Suncoast PBA endorsement (campaign endorsement alone is not enough). christian_liberty q0 TRUE only on explicit public profession — Richardson (Bethel AME trustee/usher) + Williams-Cox (40-yr Bethel Missionary Baptist) cleared; Hanewicz / Harting / Floyd left NULL (absence of profession is NULL, NOT FALSE). refuse_state_overreach q0 FALSE only when an official explicitly aligned with the state on a conservative-rubric mandate (Richardson said 'It would not be in my best interest to put myself in a position to potentially be removed from office' re: 287(g) — that's COMPLIANCE on a conservative-coded mandate, scored FALSE). Floyd's pushback against DeSantis preemption on DEI / Pride / tenant ordinance is directionally leftward and NOT conservative-rubric coded — held NULL there but scored on the substantive cells where his statements are direct evidence. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable URL; null where no evidence; never inferred from party label or DSA affiliation.

set.office: City Council, District 4, City of St. Petersburg, Florida (first elected Nov 2, 2021; sworn in Jan 6, 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025; current term to Jan 2030; serving as 2026 Council Chair; first Latina/Hispanic elected to St. Pete City Council)

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — official stpete.org bio and lissetforstpete.com 'Meet Lisset' page silent on religion; no public profession of faith located
profile.background: Lisset Hanewicz — former Pinellas-Pasco Assistant State Attorney and former Assistant U.S. Attorney (M.D. Fla.); runs a private law practice since 2014. Born in Tampa to Cuban-exile parents (Freedom Flights); first in family to attend college. BA Psychology (FIU 1999), MBA (FIU 2001), JD cum laude (UF Levin 2004). Crescent Lake neighborhood resident since 2006 with husband Wayne and one daughter; prior Crescent Lake Neighborhood Association president. First elected Nov 2 2021 (Suncoast PBA + Ruth's List endorsed); sworn in Jan 6 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025; serving as 2026 Council Chair (succeeded Copley Gerdes).

lisset-hanewicz.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 4 Councilmember and 2026 Council Chair (stpete.org D4 bio + Florida Politics chair-gavel coverage + Tampa Bay Times Nov 2025 unopposed-re-election report). Sweep of stpete.org, lissetforstpete.com, Florida Politics, St. Pete Catalyst, Watermark Online, Fox 13, Bay News 9, San Pedro Gazette, Ruth's List FL. HARD evidence — 0 TRUE / 3 FALSE / 27 NULL after exhaustive sweep. FALSE: sanctity_of_life.1 (Ruth's List Florida — Democratic women's PAC for pro-abortion-rights candidates — endorsed her 2021 election + lists her as alumni for the 2025 cycle; affirmative pro-abortion-PAC alignment). biblical_marriage.4 (as 2026 Council Chair Hanewicz delivered the Pride flag-raising remarks at City Hall both June 2025 and June 2026 — 'Hate thrives when people are isolated, silenced, or made to feel invisible' / 'You belong... in this city, you belong at the table' — Pride symbolism elevated in her official capacity; on the Oct 2025 Driscoll Harmony/History flag resolution she raised state-preemption concerns without opposing the flags themselves and the resolution was withdrawn before a final roll call, but the pattern is sustained pro-Pride). economic_stewardship.2 (voted AYE on the FY2025-26 St. Pete budget at 6.4525 millage — 3.93% above rollback — without on-record restraint advocacy; only Driscoll + Givens dissented). NULL HOLDS (downgraded per skill discipline): christian_liberty.0 NULL — no public Christian profession located on bio or campaign site; absence of profession is NULL, NOT FALSE (rubric rule). biblical_marriage.0 NULL — her 2026 'You belong in our houses of worship' Pride remarks affirm LGBTQ inclusion broadly but do not include an explicit redefinition-of-marriage statement; the q4 cell already captures the Pride symbolism. public_justice.0 NULL — Suncoast PBA endorsed her in 2021 but campaign endorsements alone for a member who has not run a sustained pro-LE program do not clear the high-bar; honest hold. border_immigration ALL NULL — no on-record statement on SPPD 287(g) located despite the Feb 2026 council public-comment campaign; her Feb 2026 Chair role was procedural decorum, not a substantive position (San Pedro Gazette). No found evidence on sanctity_of_life q0/2/3/4, biblical_marriage q1/2/3, family_child_sovereignty (any), christian_liberty q1/2/3/4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1/2/3/4, refuse_state_overreach (any), border_immigration (any), or self_defense (any) — all NULL.

1.note: Ruth's List Florida (Democratic women's PAC supporting pro-abortion-rights candidates) endorsed her 2021 election and lists her as alumni for her 2025 re-election cycle

4.note: As 2026 Council Chair Hanewicz personally delivered the Pride flag-raising remarks at St. Pete City Hall (continuing the city's 12+ year tradition) and addressed the LGBTQ community with 'You belong... in this city, you belong at the table' — Pride symbolism elevated in her official capacity

2.note: Voted AYE on the FY2025-26 St. Petersburg budget at 6.4525 millage rate (3.93% above rollback) without on-record restraint advocacy; only Driscoll + Givens dissented (Givens for MORE spending) — Hanewicz on the above-rollback majority

set.office: City Council, District 3, City of St. Petersburg, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 runoff over Pete Boland with 50.38%; sworn in Jan 2, 2025; term to Jan 4, 2029; succeeded term-limited Republican Ed Montanari)

profile.background: Mike Harting — founder/CEO of 3 Daughters Brewing (named for daughters Meadow, Livvi, Madi with wife Leigh). University of Florida finance graduate; former Outback Steakhouse JVP; co-owner of Bella Brava; USF adjunct professor. Father retired as Lt. Col. (1st Cavalry, Vietnam vet); family moved to St. Pete via MacDill AFB in the 1980s. Registered without party affiliation; endorsement coalition spanned R (former Mayor Rick Baker, ex-Sen. Jeff Brandes, Blackmon) and D (Sen. Darryl Rouson, Councilmember Driscoll). Narrowly defeated Pete Boland Nov 5 2024 runoff to replace term-limited Republican Ed Montanari; sworn in Jan 2, 2025.

mike-harting.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 3 Councilmember (stpete.org D3 bio + Florida Politics Nov 2024 runoff coverage + Bay News 9 swearing-in). Sweep of stpete.org, mike4stpete.com, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia, Bay News 9, WUSF, St. Pete Catalyst, Tampa Bay Business & Wealth, San Pedro Gazette. HARD evidence — 1 TRUE / 1 FALSE / 28 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: biblical_marriage.4 — Oct 9 2025 Harting was the LONE NO vote on Driscoll's Harmony (Pride colors) + History flag resolution, arguing the existing city flag should represent all 280,000 St. Pete residents equally and that 'inclusive' was being misapplied to elevate two specific subgroups; the resolution was withdrawn before a final binding roll-call but his on-the-record opposition is documented in both WUSF and St. Pete Catalyst coverage — clears TRUE per the rubric. FALSE: economic_stewardship.2 — voted AYE on the FY2025-26 budget at 6.4525 millage (3.93% above rollback) without on-record restraint advocacy at adoption; only Driscoll + Givens dissented; his Dec 2025 'budget stress test' request post-dated the adoption and was reactive to anticipated state property-tax cuts (St. Pete Catalyst). NULL HOLDS: christian_liberty.0 NULL — no public Christian profession in bio, campaign site, or press coverage; absence of profession is NULL, NOT FALSE. public_justice.0 NULL — no affirmative sustained pro-LE program in first ~18 months in seat; honest hold. border_immigration NULL — no on-record statement on SPPD 287(g) located despite Feb 2026 public-comment campaign. religion NULL — registered without party affiliation, no faith profession found. All other 26 cells NULL after sweep — no statements on life, marriage q0-3, family/schools, religious liberty, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, broader border, or 2A located.

4.note: Oct 9, 2025 — Harting was the LONE NO vote on Driscoll's Harmony (Pride colors) + History flag resolution, arguing the existing city flag should represent all 280,000 St. Pete residents equally and that 'inclusive' was being misapplied to elevate two specific subgroups (Pride + Black-history). Resolution withdrawn before final binding roll-call, but his on-record opposition is documented

2.note: Voted AYE on the FY2025-26 budget at 6.4525 millage (3.93% above rollback); only Driscoll + Givens dissented. His Dec 2025 'budget stress test' request post-dated adoption and was reactive to anticipated state-level property-tax cuts, not pre-adoption restraint advocacy

set.office: City Council, District 8, City of St. Petersburg, Florida (first elected Nov 2021 over Jeff Danner; sworn in Jan 2022; serving as 2026 Council Vice-Chair; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025; current term to Jan 2030; press has described him as Florida's 'first open socialist elected in over a century')

profile.background: Richie Floyd (b. ~1991) — Fort Walton Beach, FL native. BS University of West Florida (2014); graduate degree University of York (UK, 2018). Career: systems engineer at Honeywell Aerospace; Pinellas County Schools science teacher. Democratic Socialists of America-endorsed; Pinellas County Democratic Party endorsed; SEIU FL endorsed. First elected Nov 2021 in narrow win over Jeff Danner; sworn in as 2026 Council Vice-Chair alongside Chair Hanewicz; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025 to a four-year term to 2030. Press calls him St. Pete's youngest councilmember and Florida's 'first open socialist elected in a century.'

richie-floyd.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 8 Councilmember and 2026 Vice-Chair (stpete.org + Florida Politics gavel coverage + Tampa Bay Times unopposed-re-election report). Sweep of stpete.org, richiefloyd.com, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia, St. Pete Catalyst, Creative Loafing Tampa Bay (cltampa), Watermark, WUSF, Fox 13, WTSP, Axios Tampa Bay, San Pedro Gazette, Protean Mag, SEIU FL. HARD evidence — 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE / 24 NULL after exhaustive sweep. FALSE: sanctity_of_life.0 — Floyd introduced + voted YES on $50K of city general fund money to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund (lost 6-2; Floyd + Figgs-Sanders in favor) AND authored St. Pete's reproductive-rights resolution opposing criminalization of abortion (CL Tampa, Fox 13, WUSF). biblical_marriage.4 — voted with Oct 9, 2025 majority to advance Driscoll's Harmony + History flag proposal and publicly praised it as 'a creative idea that continues to push forward representation for LGBTQ and African American communities' (WUSF, WTSP). economic_stewardship.2 — voted AYE on FY2025-26 budget at 6.4525 millage (above rollback); publicly opposes property-tax cuts as benefiting wealth, calls property tax 'a wealth tax,' and campaigns for replacing private Duke Energy with municipal utility (St. Pete Catalyst, St. Pete Standard). public_justice.0 — championed alternative-response CAL (Community Assistance Liaison) social-worker program as an activist before election; framed armed-police response as escalating; affirmative anti-LE-advocacy posture (Florida Politics, CL Tampa). public_justice.3 — frames non-violent 911 calls (mental-health, addiction, homelessness) as public-health / social-work matters appropriately routed away from armed police response; embraces the public-health intervention model (CL Tampa). border_immigration.2 — on record to Axios Tampa Bay: 'Abolish ICE, end 287(g)' and 'If there is a legal path to ending it then I 100% support it'; called federal immigration agents 'jack-booted thugs'; active opposition to SPPD's 287(g) partnership (Axios, San Pedro Gazette, CL Tampa). NULL HOLDS (downgraded per skill): christian_liberty.0 NULL — no Christian profession; absence is NULL not FALSE. refuse_state_overreach NULL — his fights against state preemption on DEI / Pride / tenant ordinance (HB 1417) are directionally LEFTWARD and not conservative-rubric coded; honest hold. All remaining cells NULL — no cited evidence on sanctity_of_life q1-4, biblical_marriage q0-3, family/schools, christian_liberty q1-4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1/2/4, border_immigration q0/1/3/4, self_defense (any).

0.note: Floyd introduced and voted YES on $50K of city general-fund money to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund (lost 6-2; only Floyd + Figgs-Sanders in favor) AND authored St. Pete's reproductive-rights resolution opposing criminalization of abortion — affirmative pro-abortion-funding posture

4.note: Voted with Oct 9, 2025 majority (Driscoll + Givens + Figgs-Sanders + Gerdes + Hanewicz + Floyd) to advance the Harmony (Pride colors) + History flag proposal; publicly praised it as 'a creative idea that continues to push forward representation for LGBTQ and African American communities'

2.note: Publicly opposes property-tax cuts/elimination as benefiting wealth, calls property tax 'a wealth tax,' and campaigns for replacing private Duke Energy with municipal utility; advocates expanded public spending ($50K abortion fund, social housing); voted AYE on the FY26 above-rollback budget; no restraint posture

0.note: Championed alternative-response CAL (Community Assistance Liaison) social-worker program as activist before election; criticized armed-police response as escalating — affirmative anti-pro-LE posture

3.note: Frames non-violent 911 calls (mental-health, addiction, homelessness) as public-health / social-work matters appropriately routed away from armed-police response — public-health intervention frame embraced

2.note: On record to Axios Tampa Bay: 'Abolish ICE, end 287(g)' and 'If there is a legal path to ending it then I 100% support it'; called federal immigration agents 'jack-booted thugs' — active opposition to SPPD's 287(g) ICE partnership

set.office: City Commissioner, Seat 2, City of Tallahassee, Florida (first elected 2014; re-elected 2018; re-elected Nov 2024 in runoff defeating Dot Inman-Johnson; term to Nov 2028; currently serving as Mayor Pro Tem)

profile.religion: Christian — long-time member of Bethel AME Church Tallahassee, serving as usher and trustee (publicly listed on bethel1865.org Board of Trustees roster and confirmed on talgov.com bio).
profile.background: Curtis B. Richardson (b. Oct 8, 1956, Jacksonville, FL; raised Green Cove Springs). BS Psychology (FSU); MA Counseling Psychology (UWF); MS School Psychology (FSU 1983). School psychologist Gadsden County 1983-1991; staff to FL Education Commissioner Betty Castor; Deputy Director of Cabinet Affairs under Gov. Lawton Chiles; Director of School Improvement Gadsden. Leon County School Board 1990-1996 (first Black male elected, served as Chair). Florida House of Representatives District 8 (D, Tallahassee) 4 terms 2000-2008. Tallahassee City Commission Seat 2 since 2014 (re-elected 2018, re-elected Nov 2024 over Dot Inman-Johnson). Currently Mayor Pro Tem. Married to Leon County Judge Nina Ashenafi-Richardson. Long-time member of Bethel AME Tallahassee (usher / trustee).

curtis-richardson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 2 City Commissioner and current Mayor Pro Tem (talgov.com bio + WFSU Nov 2024 re-election coverage + bethel1865.org board roster). Sweep of talgov.com, bethel1865.org, Tallahassee Reports, WCTV, WTXL, WFSU, Tallahassee Democrat, Panhandle Press, LiveAction, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 4 FALSE / 23 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 (Bethel AME Church Tallahassee usher AND trustee — explicit public Christian profession on both his city bio and the church's official board roster). public_justice.0 (sustained affirmative pro-LE advocacy clears the high-bar: led FY2024 millage hike specifically to fund 20 ADDITIONAL TPD officers and TPD technology; voted YES on $625K federal grant to hire police officers Nov 2025; publicly defended pro-police budget at conservative candidate forum — 'We raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' — not just a budget vote, an explicit pro-LE program). border_immigration.2 (Oct 22, 2025 voted AGAINST motion to rescind Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force; TPD cooperates with ICE; vote stands even though rhetoric framed reluctant compliance with state-removal threats). FALSE: sanctity_of_life.0 (explicit pro-choice on record: 'I believe in a woman's right to choose' and 'that is a choice between my wife and me'; 2021 NO vote on the city's pro-choice resolution was procedural disbelief that the Legislature would listen, NOT a pro-life vote — LiveAction + WCTV). family_child_sovereignty.3 (self-identified as 'one of the biggest opponents' of Jeb Bush's opportunity scholarship program — opposes FL school choice / voucher expansion). economic_stewardship.2 (voted YES on FY2025 budget 3-2 at 4.42 mills above rollback AND on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 mills; explicitly rejected the rollback proposal that would have required ~$3.9M of cuts — 'we raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' is honest framing, but it scores FALSE on the fiscal-restraint cell as written). refuse_state_overreach.0 (re: 287(g) said 'It would not be in my best interest to put myself in a position to potentially be removed from office' — explicit compliance posture on a conservative-rubric-coded state mandate). NULL HOLDS: biblical_marriage NULL — Tallahassee historically issued Pride proclamations under his tenure but no specific Richardson floor vote was sourced this batch; the 2026 unanimous review-of-DEI vote was compliance with state law, not affirmative marriage defense; honest hold. self_defense NULL — no sourced 2A statement post-FSU April 2025 shooting located. All other cells NULL — no cited evidence on sanctity_of_life q1-4, biblical_marriage q0-4, family q0-2,4, christian_liberty q1-4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1-4, refuse_state_overreach q1-4, border_immigration q0/1/3/4, self_defense (any).

0.note: Explicit pro-choice on record: 'I believe in a woman's right to choose' and 'that is a choice between my wife and me'; the 2021 NO vote on the city's pro-choice resolution was procedural ('the Legislature won't listen'), not a pro-life vote

3.note: Self-identified as 'one of the biggest opponents' of Jeb Bush's opportunity scholarship program (precursor to FL school choice / vouchers); opposes current school-choice expansion

0.note: Explicit public Christian profession: usher AND trustee at Bethel AME Church Tallahassee, listed on the church's official Board of Trustees roster and on his city-bio public profile

2.note: Voted YES on FY2025 budget (3-2) at 4.42 millage AND on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 millage, both above rollback; explicitly rejected the rollback proposal that would have required ~$3.9M cuts. Honest framing: 'We raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' — clears public_justice.0 but scores FALSE on the fiscal-restraint cell

0.note: Sustained affirmative pro-LE advocacy clears the high-bar: led FY2024 millage hike specifically to fund 20 ADDITIONAL TPD officers and TPD technology; voted YES on the $625K federal grant to hire officers (Nov 2025); publicly defended pro-police budget at conservative candidate forum — explicit pro-LE program, not just a budget vote

Explicit compliance posture on a conservative-rubric state mandate: stated 'It would not be in my best interest to put myself in a position to potentially be removed from office' re: 287(g) — prioritizes state-law compliance over local resistance; the leftward DEI / Pride preemption frustrations are not rubric-coded

2.note: Oct 22, 2025 voted AGAINST motion to rescind Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force, TPD cooperates with ICE. Counts even where rhetoric framed it as reluctant compliance with state-removal threats

set.office: City Commissioner, Seat 5, City of Tallahassee, Florida (SEAT CORRECTION: prior record listed Seat 1; talgov.com bio + 2026 candidate filings confirm Seat 5; first elected Nov 2018; re-elected Aug 2022 outright with 52.37%; current term to Nov 16, 2026; filed July 2025 for a third 4-year term; faces Tifany Hill, Joe Kalicki, Bernard Michael Stevens II in the Aug 2026 nonpartisan primary)

profile.religion: Christian — 40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church, Tallahassee; serves on several ministries (per City of Tallahassee official bio and FAMUAN feature).
profile.background: Dianne Williams-Cox (b. Quincy, FL). James A. Shanks HS honors graduate 1981; BS Data Processing Technology (FAMU); MBA (Nova Southeastern). Career: software developer GM; Harris Government Systems; 19 years State of Florida; FAMU adjunct professor. Currently Direct Services Quality Assurance Manager for Voices for Florida and owner of DWC Management Consulting Services. Married 33+ years to Thomas 'Tommy' Cox; three adult sons, six grandchildren. Founder, Southside United Citizens Action Alliance; former Tallahassee-Leon County Planning Commission; ran for FL House District 8 in 2014. 2018 Capital Outlook 'Community Activist of the Year.' First elected to Tallahassee City Commission Nov 2018; re-elected outright Aug 2022. Past Vice Chair Leon County DEC; past President Capital City Democratic Women's Club; Region 1 Chair Democratic Women of Florida. 40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church Tallahassee, multiple ministries.

dianne-williams-cox.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Tallahassee City Commissioner (talgov.com bio + WFSU 2026-candidate-field coverage + WCTV / Florida Politics confirmation). SEAT CORRECTION FLAG: scorecard listed her as Seat 1 but talgov.com bio and 2026 candidate filings (Kalicki + Hill challenging her in Seat 5) confirm she holds SEAT 5; office text corrected accordingly. (Matlow's seat in our scorecard may also be miscoded — defer until his future batch.) Sweep of talgov.com, bethel1865 / FAMUAN, Tallahassee Reports, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia, WCTV, WFSU, WTXL, AOL, Capital Equality. HARD evidence — 4 TRUE / 4 FALSE / 22 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 (40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church Tallahassee, serves on multiple ministries — explicit sustained public Christian profession). public_justice.0 (FL PBA Big Bend Chapter Mar 2026 endorsement cites her SUSTAINED record of approving negotiated PBA contracts with wage / benefit increases — affirmative pro-LE record with documented platform, clears the high-bar beyond a single budget vote). border_immigration.2 (Oct 22, 2025 voted 3-2 with Mayor Dailey + Commissioner Richardson AGAINST rescinding Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force). FALSE: sanctity_of_life.0 (publicly identified as pro-choice during 2022 campaign exchange over the Oct 2021 city pro-choice resolution; her NO vote on that 2021 resolution was procedural, not pro-life — Tallahassee Reports). biblical_marriage.0 (endorsed by Capital Equality Democratic Caucus in 2018 — LGBTQ-advocacy organization requiring pro-LGBTQ platform — Capital Equality endorsements page). economic_stewardship.2 (voted YES on FY2025 budget 3-2 at 4.42 mills above rollback and on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 mills; on the majority that REJECTED rollback rates Matlow + Porter advocated for). public_justice.3 (Mayor Pro Tem support of the city's $5M-over-5-years gun-violence reduction commitment framed as community-education + conflict-resolution — public-health intervention frame embraced; corrected from agent-draft TRUE → FALSE per rubric directionality). NULL HOLDS (downgraded per skill): christian_liberty.2 NULL — civic-association building (Southside United Citizens Action Alliance) is not a religious-display defense and does not map to that cell; honest hold (corrected from agent-draft TRUE → NULL). sanctity_of_life.1 NULL — no specific pro-abortion PAC endorsement (Ruth's List / EQFL) located for her, only the Capital Equality 2018 LGBTQ endorsement; honest hold (q0 already captures the pro-choice profession). biblical_marriage q1/2/3/4 NULL — no specific Pride proclamation / TDOV / pronoun-policy / drag-restriction vote located for her in this sweep. refuse_state_overreach NULL — 287(g) compliance is leftward-resistance-suppressed, not conservative-rubric resistance; honest hold. self_defense NULL — no on-record 2A or post-FSU shooting statement located. All other cells NULL after sweep — no statements on family / schools, christian_liberty q1/3/4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1/2/4, refuse_state_overreach (any), border_immigration q0/1/3/4, or self_defense (any).

0.note: Publicly identifies as pro-choice per Tallahassee Reports coverage of the Oct 2021 abortion-resolution vote + 2022 campaign exchange; her NO vote on that 2021 resolution was procedural disbelief that the Legislature would listen, not a pro-life vote
Endorsed by Capital Equality Democratic Caucus in 2018 — LGBTQ-advocacy organization whose endorsement requires a pro-LGBTQ platform

Official City of Tallahassee bio: 'Member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church in Tallahassee, serving on several ministries'; FAMUAN feature confirms 40-year sustained membership — explicit public Christian profession

2.note: Voted YES on FY2025 budget (3-2) at 4.42 mills above rollback and on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 mills; on the majority that rejected the rollback rates Matlow + Porter advocated for

0.note: FL PBA Big Bend Chapter Mar 2026 endorsement cites her SUSTAINED record of approving negotiated PBA contracts with wage / benefit increases — affirmative pro-LE program with documented platform, clears the high-bar beyond a single budget vote

3.note: Mayor Pro Tem support of the city's $5M-over-5-years gun-violence reduction commitment framed as community-education + conflict-resolution — public-health intervention frame embraced

2.note: Oct 22, 2025 voted with Mayor Dailey + Commissioner Richardson (3-2) AGAINST rescinding Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force
